excluded_summary: Implementation code and test results from the completed hypothesis layer are excluded.
GitHub issue discussions and private planning notes are excluded.
extraction_loss: Requirements are represented as structured records; prose rationale is compressed.
Dogfood run results are captured as a single test record; the full obstruction frequency table is not replicated.
Usability notes are compressed into a single evidence source; individual observer sessions are not listed.
trust_notes: Synthetic dogfood fixture for hypothesis validation in requirements definition.
The engagement subject is AdvisoryGraphen's own hypothesis-first workflow feature requirements.

records.title: Hypothesis Layer
records.summary: Feature that adds hypothesis cells, lifecycle commands, and proposal-trace invariants to AdvisoryGraphen.

records.title: Dogfood Fixture Corpus
records.summary: Set of synthetic AdvisoryGraphen fixtures used to validate the tool against its own advisory outputs.

records.title: Product Lead
records.summary: Accountable for AdvisoryGraphen product scope and requirements decisions.

records.title: Check report must output competing hypotheses per obstruction
records.summary: For each obstruction in a check report, the system must emit the set of competing hypotheses that could explain it, including the primary hypothesis and alternatives.

records.title: CLI must support symmetric hypothesis lifecycle commands
records.summary: The CLI must provide hypothesis support, falsify, accept, and reject commands so advisors can move hypotheses through their review lifecycle from the terminal.

records.title: Actions must be traceable to source hypotheses
records.summary: Each action in a developer_action projection must include the hypothesis that motivated it, so advisors and reviewers can audit the reasoning chain from observation to recommendation.

records.title: Missing-owner obstructions are the primary advisory signal in practice
records.summary: Real advisory engagements most frequently produce missing_owner obstructions, not boundary_violation. Coverage should prioritise ownership gap hypotheses over architectural boundary hypotheses.

expected_observations: missing_owner obstructions appear in every real dogfood fixture
boundary_violation appears only in the synthetic direct-db-access fixture
falsifiers: boundary_violation obstructions appear in non-synthetic real-engagement fixtures
missing_owner frequency drops in mature organisations with explicit ownership policies

records.title: Boundary-violation obstructions are the primary advisory signal in practice
records.summary: Architectural boundary violations drive the most advisory findings. Cross-context database access is a common antipattern that the hypothesis layer should address first.

expected_observations: boundary_violation obstructions appear in real customer engagement snapshots
direct database access antipattern is observable in non-synthetic fixtures
falsifiers: dogfood corpus shows boundary_violation only in one synthetic fixture
real dogfood fixtures emit missing_owner and requirement_unverified, not boundary_violation

records.title: Advisors need symmetric lifecycle commands to complete hypothesis reviews
records.summary: Without a CLI for hypothesis support/falsify/accept/reject, advisors cannot close the hypothesis review loop without workarounds. Lifecycle symmetry is required for practical adoption.

expected_observations: advisors ask for lifecycle commands in usability sessions
review sessions stall without a clear command for recording hypothesis outcomes
falsifiers: advisors complete full hypothesis reviews using only completions accept/reject
lifecycle commands remain unused after introduction in follow-up sessions

records.title: Dogfood obstruction frequency analysis
records.summary: Run lift and check across all dogfood fixtures; record obstruction types and hypothesis emission counts. The result table in docs/19 is the authoritative evidence.

metadata.verification_method: run all dogfood fixtures through lift + check and inspect obstruction frequency table

records.title: CLI acceptance tests for hypothesis lifecycle commands
records.summary: End-to-end acceptance tests verifying that hypothesis support, falsify, accept, and reject commands produce correct review events and project outputs.

records.title: Implement hypothesis generation for missing-owner obstructions
records.summary: Add competing hypotheses and falsifiers for missing_owner obstructions to the check report. The primary hypothesis is that the owner is unassigned because no team currently holds the action.

metadata.required_verification: dogfood fixtures that previously emitted missing_owner obstructions now emit 3 competing hypotheses each

records.title: Implement hypothesis lifecycle CLI commands
records.summary: Add hypothesis support, falsify, accept, and reject commands to the CLI. Commands must append review events to the case log and update hypothesis lifecycle status.

records.title: Run advisor usability study for hypothesis review workflow
records.summary: Conduct structured usability sessions with 3+ advisors to observe whether hypothesis lifecycle commands are adopted or bypassed. Use findings to falsify or support the lifecycle-symmetry hypothesis.

metadata.required_verification: study report documents at least 3 advisor sessions with outcome classification

records.title: Dogfood run verifies competing hypotheses output requirement
records.summary: The dogfood frequency analysis confirms that check reports emit competing hypotheses for missing_owner and requirement_unverified obstructions.

records.title: Dogfood analysis supports missing-owner hypothesis
records.summary: The dogfood obstruction frequency table shows missing_owner in every real fixture, supporting the claim that it is the primary advisory signal.

records.title: Dogfood analysis falsifies boundary-violation hypothesis
records.summary: The dogfood run shows boundary_violation obstructions appear only in the synthetic direct-db-access fixture; real fixtures show none. This falsifies the hypothesis that boundary violations are the primary signal.

records.title: Missing-owner hypothesis competes with boundary-violation hypothesis
records.summary: These two hypotheses offer competing explanations for which obstruction type most warrants hypothesis layer coverage. Dogfood resolved the competition in favour of missing-owner.

records.title: Product lead owns implement-missing-owner action
records.summary: The product lead is accountable for the missing-owner hypothesis layer implementation decision.

records.title: Product lead owns usability study action
records.summary: The product lead is accountable for commissioning and reviewing the advisor usability study.

records.title: Implement-missing-owner action derives from primary owner-gap hypothesis
records.summary: The implementation action for missing-owner hypothesis generation is derived from the strongly-supported hypothesis that missing_owner is the primary advisory signal.

records.title: Implement-lifecycle-commands action derives from lifecycle-symmetry hypothesis
records.summary: The CLI lifecycle commands implementation derives from the candidate hypothesis that advisors need symmetric lifecycle commands. The hypothesis is not yet supported by evidence.

records.title: Usability study action derives from lifecycle-symmetry hypothesis
records.summary: The advisor usability study is proposed to collect evidence that would support or falsify the lifecycle-symmetry hypothesis.

metadata.purpose: Dogfood AdvisoryGraphen hypothesis validation on its own requirements definition for the hypothesis-first workflow feature.
